denial_pattern_sources: contraindication_to_step_therapy: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey; State step-therapy exception laws (NY, TX, IL)
algo_boilerplate_fingerprint: JAMA Health Forum Feb 2024 (AI in coverage decisions); ProPublica 'Uncovered' Series 2023
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 10/16/2026 for A1 pulley release related to Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied Cigna Medical Coverage Policies and Milliman Care Guidelines (MCG). The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Annual visit limit for services treating Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30) has been exceeded. Additional visits are not a covered benefit this plan year.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with visit limit exceeded. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

Sincerely,

Cigna Health Care Management
clinical_context: The 49-year-old member has Trigger finger, ring finger (M65.30). She has been treated under commercial coverage with A1 pulley release recommended as the next appropriate step. Locking daily; failed steroid injection twice. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that visit limit exceeded bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
